title: Cedarburg Bog

infobox.value: This article about a location in [Ozaukee County, Wisconsin](/wiki/Ozaukee_County,_Wisconsin "Ozaukee County, Wisconsin") is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Cedarburg_Bog&action=edit).

is a [bog](/wiki/Bog "Bog") located in [Ozaukee County, Wisconsin](/wiki/Ozaukee_County,_Wisconsin "Ozaukee County, Wisconsin") owned by the [Wisconsin Department of Natural Resources](/wiki/Wisconsin_Department_of_Natural_Resources "Wisconsin Department of Natural Resources") and the [University of Wisconsin–Milwaukee](/wiki/University_of_Wisconsin%E2%80%93Milwaukee "University of Wisconsin–Milwaukee").[[1]](#cite_note-1) It is the largest intact [wetland](/wiki/Wetland "Wetland") complex in southeastern [Wisconsin](/wiki/Wisconsin "Wisconsin").[[2]](#cite_note-2) The bog is a refuge and habitat for several species of [plantlife](/wiki/Plantlife "Plantlife") and [wildlife](/wiki/Wildlife "Wildlife").[[3]](#cite_note-3) It was designated a [Wisconsin State Natural Area](/wiki/Wisconsin_State_Natural_Areas_Program "Wisconsin State Natural Areas Program") in 1952 and a [National Natural Landmark](/wiki/National_Natural_Landmark "National Natural Landmark") in 1973. Additionally, it is part of the National Experimental Ecological Reserve Network.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
["Cedarburg Bog (No. 2)"](http://dnr.wi.gov/topic/lands/naturalareas/index.asp?sna=2). Wisconsin Department of Natural Resources. Retrieved January 19, 2012.

["Cedarburg Bog"](https://web.archive.org/web/20121205160548/http://www4.uwm.edu/fieldstation/naturalarea/cedarburgbog.cfm). University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee. Archived from [the original](http://www4.uwm.edu/fieldstation/naturalarea/cedarburgbog.cfm) on December 5, 2012. Retrieved January 19, 2012.

**Cedarburg Bog** is a [bog](/wiki/Bog "Bog") located in [Ozaukee County, Wisconsin](/wiki/Ozaukee_County,_Wisconsin "Ozaukee County, Wisconsin") owned by the [Wisconsin Department of Natural Resources](/wiki/Wisconsin_Department_of_Natural_Resources "Wisconsin Department of Natural Resources") and the [University of Wisconsin–Milwaukee](/wiki/University_of_Wisconsin%E2%80%93Milwaukee "University of Wisconsin–Milwaukee").[[1]](#cite_note-1) It is the largest intact [wetland](/wiki/Wetland "Wetland") complex in southeastern [Wisconsin](/wiki/Wisconsin "Wisconsin").[[2]](#cite_note-2) The bog is a refuge and habitat for several species of [plantlife](/wiki/Plantlife "Plantlife") and [wildlife](/wiki/Wildlife "Wildlife").[[3]](#cite_note-3) It was designated a [Wisconsin State Natural Area](/wiki/Wisconsin_State_Natural_Areas_Program "Wisconsin State Natural Areas Program") in 1952 and a [National Natural Landmark](/wiki/National_Natural_Landmark "National Natural Landmark") in 1973. Additionally, it is part of the National Experimental Ecological Reserve Network.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
